title: Add the "copier" package
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

> /kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Add new primitives for reading and writing data, represented as tar streams, from and to possibly-chrooted directories via subprocesses.  For now, these are library APIs that aren't being used yet.

#### How to verify it

Run the unit tests.

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None directly.

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

This is part of #2480.  Public types whose docs claim them as implementation details are public for the sake of the JSON encoder, since we pass them over pipes to subprocesses when we're using chroot().

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```
None
```

comments.body: Should this be in pkg/copier?  Would that make it easier for Podman to use it?

It has dependencies on our `util` package, while packages under `pkg` are generally expected to not need things outside of `pkg`.  I don't think podman would have a problem importing it from its current location, as it's comparable to `imagebuildah` that way.
The location should be fine - we're probably dragging anything this does in already as part `podman build`.
